title: Gallery of mascarade-generaded masks
code: library(mascarade) library(data.table) library(ggplot2) library(colorrepel)

title: Gallery of mascarade-generaded masks
text: labels:
code: example <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/pbmc3k_umap.rds")) data <- data.table(example$dims,                     cluster=example$clusters)  maskTable <- generateMask(dims=example$dims,                            clusters=example$clusters)  ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +      geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +      fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1, linewidth = 0) +     theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Gallery of mascarade-generaded masks
text: labels:
code: example <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/pbmc3k_tsne.rds")) data <- data.table(example$dims,                     cluster=example$clusters)  maskTable <- generateMask(dims=example$dims,                            clusters=example$clusters)  ggplot(data, aes(x=tSNE_1, y=tSNE_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +      geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() ggplot(data, aes(x=tSNE_1, y=tSNE_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +      fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1, linewidth = 0) +     theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Gallery of mascarade-generaded masks
code: example <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/aya.rds")) data <- data.table(example$dims,                     cluster=example$clusters)  maskTable <- generateMask(dims=example$dims,                            clusters=example$clusters)  ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster), size=0.5) +      scale_color_repel() +     geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic()

title: Gallery of mascarade-generaded masks
code: example <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/chiajung1.rds")) data <- data.table(example$dims,                     cluster=example$clusters)  maskTable <- generateMask(dims=example$dims,                            clusters=example$clusters)  ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster), size=0.1) +      scale_color_repel() +     geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() example <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/chiajung2.rds")) data <- data.table(example$dims,                     cluster=example$clusters)  maskTable <- generateMask(dims=example$dims,                            clusters=example$clusters)  ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +      scale_color_repel() +     geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic()

title: Gallery of mascarade-generaded masks
text: Really hard case, playing parameters bit make space.
code: example <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/vshitov.rds")) data <- data.table(example$dims,                     cluster=example$clusters)  maskTable <- generateMask(dims=example$dims,                            clusters=example$clusters)  ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP1, y=UMAP2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster), size=0.1) +      scale_color_repel() +     fancyMask(maskTable,                con.type = "line", # ledges clutter the image visually here               ratio=1,                linewidth = 0,                limits.expand = c(0.2, 0.1), # more space to the left and right of the plot               label.buffer = unit(1, "mm"), # smaller buffer zone around clusters               label.width = unit(25, "mm"), # wrap long lines               label.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1, "pt"), # tighter label margins               label.fontsize = 10) +     theme_classic() + theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Using mascarade package
text: package stable version can installed CRAN: recent development version package can installed GitHub:
code: install.packages("mascarade") remotes::install_github("alserglab/mascarade")

title: Using mascarade package
code: library(mascarade) library(data.table) ##  ## Attaching package: 'data.table' ## The following object is masked from 'package:base': ##  ##     %notin% library(ggplot2) library(ggforce)

title: Using mascarade package
text: Loading example data PBMC 3K processed Seurat (see details). UMAP coordinates: Cluster annotations: Expression table several genes: Let’s plot data:  Now let’s generate cluster masks: maskTable actually table cluster borders. single cluster can multiple connected parts, one single part can contain multiple border lines (groups). Now can use table draw borders geom_path (group column used group aesthetics):  can color borders instead points:  can use ggforce package make borders touch instead overlap:  presence small clusters can help expand borders bit away points.  help ggforce-based function geom_mark_shape can also put labels within plot .  can achived fancyMask() helper function:  Now can easily show association cell types expression particular genes, GNLY good marker NK cells dataset.  can focus single cluster :
code: data("exampleMascarade") head(exampleMascarade$dims) ##                   UMAP_1    UMAP_2 ## AAACATACAACCAC -4.232792 -4.152139 ## AAACATTGAGCTAC -4.892886 10.985685 ## AAACATTGATCAGC -5.508639 -7.211088 ## AAACCGTGCTTCCG 11.332233  3.161727 ## AAACCGTGTATGCG -7.450703  1.092022 ## AAACGCACTGGTAC -3.509504 -6.087042 head(exampleMascarade$clusters) ## AAACATACAACCAC AAACATTGAGCTAC AAACATTGATCAGC AAACCGTGCTTCCG AAACCGTGTATGCG  ##   Memory CD4 T              B   Memory CD4 T     CD14+ Mono             NK  ## AAACGCACTGGTAC  ##   Memory CD4 T  ## 9 Levels: Naive CD4 T Memory CD4 T CD14+ Mono B CD8 T FCGR3A+ Mono NK ... Platelet head(exampleMascarade$features) ##                     MS4A1       GNLY       CD3E      CD14     FCER1A     FCGR3A ## AAACATACAACCAC -0.4110536 -0.4081782  1.0157094 -0.393789 -0.1373491 -0.4507969 ## AAACATTGAGCTAC  2.5965712 -0.4081782 -0.9189074 -0.393789 -0.1373491 -0.4507969 ## AAACATTGATCAGC -0.4110536  0.7526607  0.8148764 -0.393789 -0.1373491 -0.4507969 ## AAACCGTGCTTCCG -0.4110536 -0.4081782 -0.9189074 -0.393789 -0.1373491  1.1300704 ## AAACCGTGTATGCG -0.4110536  2.3958265 -0.9189074 -0.393789 -0.1373491 -0.4507969 ## AAACGCACTGGTAC -0.4110536 -0.4081782  1.1029222 -0.393789 -0.1373491 -0.4507969 ##                        LYZ       PPBP       CD8A ## AAACATACAACCAC -0.11104505 -0.1416271  2.1039769 ## AAACATTGAGCTAC  0.06112027 -0.1416271 -0.3537211 ## AAACATTGATCAGC  0.07833934 -0.1416271 -0.3537211 ## AAACCGTGCTTCCG  1.40875149  2.9255239 -0.3537211 ## AAACCGTGTATGCG -0.97272094 -0.1416271 -0.3537211 ## AAACGCACTGGTAC -0.06309661 -0.1416271 -0.3537211 data <- data.table(exampleMascarade$dims,                     cluster=exampleMascarade$clusters,                    exampleMascarade$features) ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +      coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() maskTable <- generateMask(dims=exampleMascarade$dims,                            clusters=exampleMascarade$clusters) head(maskTable) ##       UMAP_1    UMAP_2           part            group      cluster ##        <num>     <num>         <char>           <char>       <fctr> ## 1: -2.581865 -7.829109 Memory CD4 T#1 Memory CD4 T#1#1 Memory CD4 T ## 2: -2.570402 -7.806183 Memory CD4 T#1 Memory CD4 T#1#1 Memory CD4 T ## 3: -2.581865 -7.783258 Memory CD4 T#1 Memory CD4 T#1#1 Memory CD4 T ## 4: -2.593327 -7.771795 Memory CD4 T#1 Memory CD4 T#1#1 Memory CD4 T ## 5: -2.616253 -7.760332 Memory CD4 T#1 Memory CD4 T#1#1 Memory CD4 T ## 6: -2.639179 -7.748869 Memory CD4 T#1 Memory CD4 T#1#1 Memory CD4 T ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +      geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(color="grey") +      geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group, color=cluster), linewidth=1) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(color="grey") +      ggforce::geom_shape(data=maskTable, aes(group=group, color=cluster),                         linewidth=1, fill=NA, expand=unit(-1, "pt")) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() maskTable <- generateMask(dims=exampleMascarade$dims,                            clusters=exampleMascarade$clusters,                           expand=0.02) ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(color="grey") +      ggforce::geom_shape(data=maskTable, aes(group=group, color=cluster),                         linewidth=1, fill=NA, expand=unit(-1, "pt")) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic() myMask <- list(     geom_mark_shape(data=maskTable, aes(group=cluster, color=cluster, label = cluster),                     fill = NA,                    linewidth=1, expand=unit(-1, "pt"),                    con.cap=0, con.type = "ledge",                    label.fontsize = 10,                     label.buffer = unit(2, "mm"),                    label.fontface = "plain",                    label.minwidth = 0,                    label.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2, "pt"),                    label.lineheight = 0,                    con.colour = "inherit",                    show.legend = FALSE),     # expanding to give a bit more space for labels     scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.1)),     scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.1)) )  ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +     geom_point(color="grey") +     myMask +     coord_fixed() +     theme_classic() ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +     geom_point(color="grey") +     fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1, cols = scales::hue_pal()) +     theme_classic() ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=GNLY), size=0.5) +     scale_color_gradient2(low = "#404040", high="red") +      fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1, cols = scales::hue_pal()) +     theme_classic() ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +      geom_point(aes(color=GNLY), size=0.5) +      scale_color_gradient2(low = "#404040", high="red") +      geom_path(data=maskTable[cluster=="NK"], aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +      theme_classic()

title: Using mascarade package
text: part vignette need Seuratpackage. Let’s get example PBMC3K dataset: object can obtained using SeuratData package (can installed remotes::install_github('satijalab/seurat-data')): Generate masks using helper function: can use fancyMask() now, reuses colors DimPlot automatically:  DimPlot, borders can viewed redundant removed:  Let’s plot NK cell marker:  multiple markers (skipping labels save space, adding colors):  Works t-SNE :
code: library(Seurat) pbmc3k <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/pbmc3k_seurat5.rds")) pbmc3k <- NormalizeData(pbmc3k) pbmc3k ## An object of class Seurat  ## 13714 features across 2638 samples within 1 assay  ## Active assay: RNA (13714 features, 2000 variable features) ##  2 layers present: counts, data ##  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap if (requireNamespace("SeuratData")) {     if (!AvailableData()["pbmc3k", "Installed"]) {         InstallData("pbmc3k")         }     LoadData("pbmc3k")          pbmc3k <- UpdateSeuratObject(pbmc3k.final)         pbmc3k } maskTable <- generateMaskSeurat(pbmc3k) DimPlot(pbmc3k) + NoLegend() +     fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1) DimPlot(pbmc3k) + NoLegend() +     fancyMask(maskTable, linewidth = 0, ratio=1) FeaturePlot(pbmc3k, "GNLY", cols=c("grey90", "red")) +     fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1) featureList <- c("MS4A1", "GNLY", "CD3E", "CD14") FeaturePlot(pbmc3k, features=featureList, cols=c("grey90", "red")) *     fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1, linewidth=0.5, label=FALSE, cols = scales::hue_pal()) pbmc3k <- RunTSNE(pbmc3k)  maskTable <- generateMaskSeurat(pbmc3k, reduction = "tsne")  FeaturePlot(pbmc3k, features=featureList, reduction = "tsne", cols=c("grey90", "red")) *      fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1, linewidth=0.5, label=FALSE, cols = scales::hue_pal())

code: @Manual{,   title = {mascarade: Generating Cluster Masks for Single-Cell Dimensional Reduction Plots},   author = {Alexey Sergushichev},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.1},   url = {https://alserglab.github.io/mascarade/}, }

what: Generating Cluster Masks for Single-Cell Dimensional Reduction Plots
title: Generating Cluster Masks for Single-Cell Dimensional Reduction Plots
text: mascarade package implements procedure automatically generate 2D masks clusters single-cell dimensional reduction plots like t-SNE UMAP. See tutorial usage details gallery examples different datasets.

title: Generating Cluster Masks for Single-Cell Dimensional Reduction Plots
text: package stable version can installed CRAN: recent development version can installed GitHub:
code: install.packages("mascarade") remotes::install_github("alserglab/mascarade")

title: Generating Cluster Masks for Single-Cell Dimensional Reduction Plots
text: Loading necessary libraries: Loading get example PBMC3K dataset: Generating masks: DimPlot mask labels:  DimPlot just labels  FeaturePlot mask labels showing GNLY gene specific NK cells:
code: library(mascarade) library(ggplot2) library(data.table) library(Seurat) pbmc3k <- readRDS(url("https://alserglab.wustl.edu/files/mascarade/examples/pbmc3k_seurat5.rds")) pbmc3k <- NormalizeData(pbmc3k) pbmc3k ## An object of class Seurat  ## 13714 features across 2638 samples within 1 assay  ## Active assay: RNA (13714 features, 2000 variable features) ##  2 layers present: counts, data ##  2 dimensional reductions calculated: pca, umap maskTable <- generateMaskSeurat(pbmc3k) DimPlot(pbmc3k) + NoLegend() +     fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1) DimPlot(pbmc3k) + NoLegend() +     fancyMask(maskTable, linewidth=0, ratio=1) FeaturePlot(pbmc3k, "GNLY", cols=c("grey90", "red")) +     fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1)

what: Build the box-fit R-tree for the cluster polygons — buildBoxFit
title: Build the box-fit R-tree for the cluster polygons — buildBoxFit
text: Constructs Boost.Geometry R-tree cluster polygon envelopes (keeps polygons) candidate polish kernels can answer "label box overlap cluster?" quickly. Rebuilt per placement; mask .

title: Build the box-fit R-tree for the cluster polygons — buildBoxFit
code: buildBoxFit(polysx, polysy)

title: Build the box-fit R-tree for the cluster polygons — buildBoxFit
text: polysx, polysy Lists parallel numeric x/y vectors, one ring per cluster.

title: Build the box-fit R-tree for the cluster polygons — buildBoxFit
text: external-pointer (XPtr<BoxFit>) handle box-fit structure.

what: Effective length used to rank label candidates — effectiveLength
title: Effective length used to rank label candidates — effectiveLength
text: Per candidate, length optimizer minimises conflict counts: base leader length, plus arc leader inside FOREIGN cluster (routes leaders around clusters), plus OVERFLOW_WEIGHT times far box overflows viewport (steers labels -bounds).

title: Effective length used to rank label candidates — effectiveLength
code: effectiveLength(   len,   ex,   ey,   tx,   ty,   lab,   polysx,   polysy,   cxmin,   cxmax,   cymin,   cymax,   xlo,   xhi,   ylo,   yhi )

title: Effective length used to rank label candidates — effectiveLength
text: len Numeric base leader length per candidate. ex, ey Numeric leader start (anchor) per candidate. tx, ty Numeric leader target (pole) per candidate. lab Integer 1-indexed -cluster candidate. polysx, polysy Lists parallel numeric x/y vectors, one ring per cluster. cxmin, cxmax, cymin, cymax Numeric padded box extents per candidate. xlo, xhi, ylo, yhi Numeric viewport bounds (already inset label.buffer).

title: Effective length used to rank label candidates — effectiveLength
text: Numeric vector effective lengths, one per candidate.

what: Example data with UMAP points from PBMC3K dataset. — exampleMascarade
title: Example data with UMAP points from PBMC3K dataset. — exampleMascarade
text: object list three elements: dims – matrix UMAP coordinates cells, clusters – vector cell population annotations, features – matrix withgene expression several genes.

what: Generate ggplot2 layers for a labeled cluster mask — fancyMask
title: Generate ggplot2 layers for a labeled cluster mask — fancyMask
text: Convenience helper returns list ggplot2 components draws polygon-like outlines places cluster labels. plotting limits expanded (via limits.expand) provide extra room labels.

title: Generate ggplot2 layers for a labeled cluster mask — fancyMask
code: fancyMask(   maskTable,   ratio = NULL,   limits.expand = ifelse(label, 0.1, 0.05),   linewidth = 1,   shape.expand = linewidth * unit(-1, "pt"),   cols = "auto",   label = TRUE,   label.largest = TRUE,   label.fontsize = 10,   label.buffer = unit(2, "mm"),   label.hardpad = unit(0, "pt"),   label.softpad = unit(6, "pt"),   label.fontface = "plain",   label.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2, "pt"),   label.width = NULL,   simp_ratio = 0.001,   con.type = "ledge" )

title: Generate ggplot2 layers for a labeled cluster mask — fancyMask
text: maskTable data.frame mask coordinates. first two columns interpreted x/y coordinates (order). Must contain least columns cluster (factor) group (grouping identifier passed geom_mark_shape()). ratio Optional aspect ratio passed ggplot2::coord_cartesian(). Use 1 equal scaling. Default NULL (fixed ratio). limits.expand Numeric scalar giving fraction x/y range expand sides setting plot limits. Default 0.1 labels 0.05 labels. linewidth Line width passed geom_mark_shape() outline. Default 1. shape.expand Expansion contraction applied marked shapes, passed geom_mark_shape(expand = ...). Default unit(-linewidth, "pt"). cols Color specification cluster outlines (labels). One : "auto" (default) — inspects plot time fancyMask() added +. layer maps colour discrete (non-numeric) variable, mask joins scale via aes(colour = cluster) colours stay sync regardless scale_color_*() order. Otherwise (continuous colour, constant colour, colour aesthetic) explicit colours scales::hue_pal() baked plot's scale system left untouched. "inherit" — always maps colour aesthetic (aes(colour = cluster)), unconditionally joining whatever colour scale present. Useful want force scale sharing; error existing scale continuous. palette function accepts single integer n returns n colors (e.g., scales::hue_pal(), rainbow). single color string — applied every cluster. unnamed character vector length equal number clusters — colors assigned clusters factor-level order. named character vector — names must match cluster levels; order matter. label Boolean flag whether labels displayed. label.largest Boolean flag. TRUE (default), largest part cluster labelled; smaller disconnected parts drawn labelled. FALSE, parts labelled. Ignored label = FALSE. label.fontsize Label font size passed geom_mark_shape(). Default 10. label.buffer Polygon padding passed geom_mark_shape(): cluster polygons dilated distance labels kept dilated zone, label keeps gap cluster outline. Default unit(2, "mm"); unit(0, "mm") disables. label.hardpad Hard box clearance passed geom_mark_shape(): label box grown padding placement decisions, labels keep least gap . Default unit(0, "pt") (label margin usually suffices; raise mainly con.type = "box"). label.softpad Extra box spacing polish step aims , top label.hardpad, passed geom_mark_shape(). Default unit(6, "pt"). label.fontface Label font face passed geom_mark_shape(). Default "plain". label.margin Label margin passed geom_mark_shape(). Default margin(2, 2, 2, 2, "pt"). label.width Soft target width wrapping labels, passed geom_mark_shape(). grid unit (e.g. unit(30, "mm")); labels balanced across lines keep line widths even close width, without short dangling line. NULL (default) leaves labels unwrapped. See geom_mark_shape() details. simp_ratio Fraction polygon bounding-box area used simplify cluster polygons label placement: small inward (concave) vertices whose cut-area simp_ratio * bbox_area removed, speeds box-fit leader-routing geometry (scale vertex count). simplified polygon encloses original, labels never overlap real cluster. Larger values simplify ; set 0 disable. Default 0.001. con.type Leader / label-mark style passed geom_mark_shape(): one "ledge", "line", "box", "none" (see geom_mark_shape() Details). Default "ledge".

title: Generate ggplot2 layers for a labeled cluster mask — fancyMask
text: list ggplot2 components suitable adding plot +, containing ggplot2::coord_cartesian() specification geom_mark_shape() layer.

title: Generate ggplot2 layers for a labeled cluster mask — fancyMask
text: first two columns maskTable used x/y coordinates. Cluster labels taken maskTable$cluster. Shapes grouped maskTable$group.

title: Generate ggplot2 layers for a labeled cluster mask — fancyMask
code: data("exampleMascarade") maskTable <- generateMask(dims=exampleMascarade$dims,                           clusters=exampleMascarade$clusters) library(ggplot2) basePlot <- ggplot(do.call(cbind, exampleMascarade)) +     geom_point(aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2, color=GNLY)) +     scale_color_gradient2(low = "#404040", high="red") +     theme_classic()  basePlot + fancyMask(maskTable, ratio=1, cols=scales::hue_pal())

what: Visible leader end (first mask-boundary hit) — firstLeaderHit
title: Visible leader end (first mask-boundary hit) — firstLeaderHit
text: label, leader runs box anchor (ex, ey) cluster pole (tx, ty), lies inside cluster. drawn leader stops mask boundary: first point anchor->pole segment crosses label's cluster ring. segment cross ring leader runs way pole.

title: Visible leader end (first mask-boundary hit) — firstLeaderHit
code: firstLeaderHit(ex, ey, tx, ty, polysx, polysy)

title: Visible leader end (first mask-boundary hit) — firstLeaderHit
text: ex, ey Numeric leader-start (anchor) coordinates, one per label. tx, ty Numeric pole (leader target) coordinates, one per label. polysx, polysy Lists parallel numeric x/y vectors, one ring per label (cluster).

title: Visible leader end (first mask-boundary hit) — firstLeaderHit
text: list numeric bx, : visible leader end, one per label.

title: Continuous force-directed label polish — forcePolish
text: Pattern-search descent (squared) EFFECTIVE length hard conflict guard. effective length label centre--pole leader length, plus arc leader runs inside foreign cluster (routing leaders around clusters), plus soft viewport overflow penalty – quantity upstream effectiveLength() ranking minimises. box-box spacing penalty added top. Starting conflict-free layout search accepts conflict-free neighbours (free-space check via BoxFit R-tree), feasibility preserved. Overflow SOFT term folded effective length, hard clip, -panel label can walked back -bounds label leaves panel lowers total energy. leader anchor rule mirrors R's .anchorPoint(), conflict guard foreign-cluster arc match drawn geometry.

title: Continuous force-directed label polish — forcePolish
code: forcePolish(   boxfit,   cx0,   cy0,   hw,   hh,   tx,   ty,   polysx,   polysy,   pad,   xlo,   xhi,   ylo,   yhi,   iters,   step,   MU,   pad_tgt,   stepmin,   con_type,   sq = TRUE )

title: Continuous force-directed label polish — forcePolish
text: boxfit External pointer buildBoxFit() (cluster keep-). cx0, cy0 Numeric starting label-centre coordinates. hw, hh Numeric per-label box half-sizes. tx, ty Numeric per-label pole (leader target). polysx, polysy Lists parallel numeric x/y vectors, one true mask ring per cluster (aligned labels), used foreign-cluster arc term. pad Numeric hard box clearance. xlo, xhi, ylo, yhi Numeric viewport bounds. iters Integer iteration count. step Numeric initial pattern-search step. MU Numeric weight box-box spacing penalty. pad_tgt Numeric target inter-box spacing. stepmin Numeric smallest step tried abandoning direction. con_type Integer leader style: 0 = "ledge" (corner), otherwise "line"/"box"/"none". sq Logical; TRUE, length term uses squared distance.

title: Continuous force-directed label polish — forcePolish
text: list numeric cx, cy: polished label centres.

what: Generate mask for clusters on 2D dimensional reduction plots — generateMask
title: Generate mask for clusters on 2D dimensional reduction plots — generateMask
text: Internally function rasterizes smoothes density plots.

title: Generate mask for clusters on 2D dimensional reduction plots — generateMask
code: generateMask(   dims,   clusters,   gridSize = 200,   expand = 0.005,   minDensity = lifecycle::deprecated(),   smoothSigma = NA,   minSize = 10,   kernel = lifecycle::deprecated(),   type = lifecycle::deprecated() )

title: Generate mask for clusters on 2D dimensional reduction plots — generateMask
text: dims matrix point coordinates. Rows points, columns dimensions. first two columns used. clusters vector cluster annotations. length number rows dims. gridSize target width height raster used internally expand distance used expand borders, represented fraction sqrt(width*height). Default: 1/200. minDensity Deprecated. anything. smoothSigma Deprecated. Parameter controlling smoothing joining close cells groups, represented fraction sqrt(width*height). Increasing parameter can help dealing sparse regions. minSize Groups less minSize points ignored, unless group cluster kernel Deprecated. anything. type Deprecated. anything.

title: Generate mask for clusters on 2D dimensional reduction plots — generateMask
text: data.table points representing mask borders. individual border line corresponds single level group column. Cluster assignment cluster column. Within cluster, parts ordered decreasing polygon area part index 1 always largest disconnected component.

title: Generate mask for clusters on 2D dimensional reduction plots — generateMask
code: data("exampleMascarade") maskTable <- generateMask(dims=exampleMascarade$dims,                           clusters=exampleMascarade$clusters) data <- data.frame(exampleMascarade$dims,                    cluster=exampleMascarade$clusters,                    exampleMascarade$features) library(ggplot2) ggplot(data, aes(x=UMAP_1, y=UMAP_2)) +     geom_point(aes(color=cluster)) +     geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(group=group)) +     coord_fixed() +     theme_classic()

Default: 1/200. minSize Groups less minSize points ignored, unless group cluster

title: Generates mask from a Seurat object. Requires SeuratObject package. — generateMaskSeurat
text: data.table points representing mask borders. individual border line corresponds single level group column. Cluster assignment cluster column.

title: Generates mask from a Seurat object. Requires SeuratObject package. — generateMaskSeurat
code: # only run if Seurat is installed if (require("Seurat")) {     data("pbmc_small")     maskTable <- generateMaskSeurat(pbmc_small)      library(ggplot2)     # not the best plot, see vignettes for better examples     DimPlot(pbmc_small) +         geom_path(data=maskTable, aes(x=tSNE_1, y=tSNE_2, group=group)) } #> Loading required package: Seurat #> Loading required package: SeuratObject #> Loading required package: sp #> ‘SeuratObject’ was built under R 4.6.0 but the current version is #> 4.6.1; it is recomended that you reinstall ‘SeuratObject’ as the ABI #> for R may have changed #>  #> Attaching package: ‘SeuratObject’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #>  #>     intersect, t

what: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
text: geom lets annotate sets points via polygonal shapes. Unlike ggforce::geom_mark_* functions, geom_mark_shape explicitly provided shape coordinates. ggforce::geom_shape, polygon can expanded/contracted corners can rounded, controlled expand radius parameters.

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
code: geom_mark_shape(   mapping = NULL,   data = NULL,   stat = "identity",   position = "identity",   expand = 0,   radius = 0,   label.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2, "mm"),   label.width = NULL,   label.minwidth = 0,   label.hjust = 0,   label.fontsize = 12,   label.family = "",   label.lineheight = 1,   label.fontface = c("bold", "plain"),   label.fill = "white",   label.colour = "black",   label.buffer = unit(10, "mm"),   label.hardpad = unit(0, "pt"),   label.softpad = unit(6, "pt"),   con.colour = "black",   con.size = 0.5,   con.type = "ledge",   con.linetype = 1,   con.border = "one",   con.cap = unit(3, "mm"),   con.arrow = NULL,   simp_ratio = 0.001,   ...,   na.rm = FALSE,   show.legend = NA,   inherit.aes = TRUE )

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
text: mapping Set aesthetic mappings created aes(). specified inherit.aes = TRUE (default), combined default mapping top level plot. must supply mapping plot mapping. data data displayed layer. three options: NULL, default, data inherited plot data specified call ggplot(). data.frame, object, override plot data. objects fortified produce data frame. See fortify() variables created. function called single argument, plot data. return value must data.frame, used layer data. function can created formula (e.g. ~ head(.x, 10)). stat statistical transformation use data layer. using geom_*() function construct layer, stat argument can used override default coupling geoms stats. stat argument accepts following: Stat ggproto subclass, example StatCount. string naming stat. give stat string, strip function name stat_ prefix. example, use stat_count(), give stat "count". information ways specify stat, see layer stat documentation. position position adjustment use data layer. can used various ways, including prevent overplotting improving display. position argument accepts following: result calling position function, position_jitter(). method allows passing extra arguments position. string naming position adjustment. give position string, strip function name position_ prefix. example, use position_jitter(), give position "jitter". information ways specify position, see layer position documentation. expand numeric unit vector length one, specifying expansion amount. Negative values result contraction instead. value given numeric understood proportion plot area width. radius expand specifying corner radius. label.margin margin around annotation boxes, given call ggplot2::margin(). label.width Soft target width wrapping label (description). grid unit (e.g. unit(30, "mm")). text balanced across lines line widths even close width, avoiding short dangling line; line may slightly exceed prevent orphan, -long single word never broken. soft cap: box shrinks fit wrapped text (never forced exact width). NULL (default) leaves label unwrapped. label.minwidth minimum width provide description. size label exceeds , description allowed fill much label. label.hjust horizontal justification annotation. contains two elements first used label second description. label.fontsize size text annotation. contains two elements first used label second description. label.family font family used annotation. contains two elements first used label second description. label.lineheight height line multipler fontsize. contains two elements first used label second description. label.fontface font face used annotation. contains two elements first used label second description. label.fill fill colour annotation box. Use "inherit" use fill enclosure "inherit_col" use border colour enclosure. label.colour text colour annotation. contains two elements first used label second description. Use "inherit" use border colour enclosure "inherit_fill" use fill colour enclosure. label.buffer Polygon padding: cluster polygons dilated distance labels kept dilated zone, leaving gap label cluster outline. grid unit; unit(0, "mm") disables . Default unit(10, 'mm'). label.hardpad Hard box clearance: label box grown padding placement decisions (seed slots, label-label label-leader conflict tests, polish), labels keep least gap . grid unit. Defaults unit(0, 'pt') – label margin usually gives enough separation; raise mainly con.type = 'box', drawn box outlines otherwise touch. label.softpad Soft box spacing polish step additionally aims , top label.hardpad (tighten hard conflict tests). grid unit. Default unit(6, 'pt'). con.colour colour line connecting annotation mark. Use "inherit" use border colour enclosure "inherit_fill" use fill colour enclosure. con.size width connector. Use "inherit" use border width enclosure. con.type Leader / label-mark style: one "ledge", "line", "box", "none" (see Details). Default "ledge". con.linetype linetype connector. Use "inherit" use border linetype enclosure. con.border bordertype connector. Either "one" (draw line horizontal side closest mark), "" (draw border sides), "none" (going explain one). con.cap distance mark line stop . con.arrow arrow specification connection using grid::arrow() end pointing towards mark. simp_ratio Fraction polygon bounding-box area used simplify cluster polygons label placement (removes small inward vertices; simplified polygon encloses original, labels never overlap real shape). Speeds placement geometry. Larger values simplify ; 0 disables. Default 0.001. ... arguments passed layer()'s params argument. arguments broadly fall one 4 categories . Notably, arguments position argument, aesthetics required can passed .... Unknown arguments part 4 categories ignored. Static aesthetics mapped scale, fixed value apply layer whole. example, colour = "red" linewidth = 3. geom's documentation Aesthetics section lists available options. 'required' aesthetics passed params. Please note passing unmapped aesthetics vectors technically possible, order required length guaranteed parallel input data. constructing layer using stat_*() function, ... argument can used pass parameters geom part layer. example stat_density(geom = "area", outline.type = ""). geom's documentation lists parameters can accept. Inversely, constructing layer using geom_*() function, ... argument can used pass parameters stat part layer. example geom_area(stat = "density", adjust = 0.5). stat's documentation lists parameters can accept. key_glyph argument layer() may also passed .... can one functions described key glyphs, change display layer legend. na.rm FALSE, default, missing values removed warning. TRUE, missing values silently removed. show.legend logical. layer included legends? NA, default, includes aesthetics mapped. FALSE never includes, TRUE always includes. can also named logical vector finely select aesthetics display. inherit.aes FALSE, overrides default aesthetics, rather combining . useful helper functions define data aesthetics inherit behaviour default plot specification, e.g. borders().

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
text: ggplot2 layer (ggplot2::layer) adds polygonal shape annotations plot.

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
text: con.type selects label connected cluster: "ledge" — leader box corner facing cluster, plus short horizontal ledge along box edge leader's start (default). "line" — leader box corner edge-midpoint facing cluster, ledge. "box" — placed "line", additionally outlines label's bounding box. "none" — connector drawn; label still placed "line".

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
text: geom_mark_shape understand following aesthetics (required aesthetics bold): x y filter label description color fill group size linetype alpha

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
text: geom_mark_* allow put descriptive textboxes connected mark plot, using label description aesthetics. textboxes automatically placed close mark, without obscuring datapoints layer. placement dynamic resize plot see annotation might move around areas become big enough small fit annotation. enough space annotation without overlapping data get drawn. cases try resizing plot, change size annotation, decrease buffer region around marks.

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
text: Often marks used draw attention , annotate specific features plot thus desirable marks around everything. possible simply pre-filter data used mark layer, geom_mark_* geoms also comes dedicated filter aesthetic , set, remove rows evalutates FALSE. multiple benefits using instead prefiltering. First, change data source, making code adaptable exploration. Second, data removed filter aesthetic remembered geom, annotation take care overlap removed data.

title: Annotate areas with polygonal shapes — geom_mark_shape
code: library(ggplot2) shape1 <- data.frame(     x = c(0, 3, 3, 2, 2, 1, 1, 0),     y = c(0, 0, 3, 3, 1, 1, 3, 3),     label = "U-shape",     description = "two prongs on a base" ) shape2 <- data.frame(     x = c(0, 3, 3, 0)+4,     y = c(0, 0, 3, 3),     label = "square",     description = "four equal sides" ) shape3 <- data.frame(     x = c(0, 1.5, 3, 1.5)+8,     y = c(1.5, 0, 1.5, 3),     label = "diamond",     description = "a square on its corner" ) shapes <- rbind(shape1, shape2, shape3)  # Label only ggplot(shapes, aes(x=x, y=y, label=label, color=label, fill=label)) +     geom_mark_shape() +     ylim(0, 5)   # Label with a secondary description line ggplot(shapes, aes(x=x, y=y, label=label, description=description,                    color=label, fill=label)) +     geom_mark_shape() +     ylim(0, 5)

title: Hungarian (Jonker-Volgenant) assignment — hungarian
text: O(n^3) minimum-cost assignment square cost matrix. Used boundary seed (one solve per column) match labels stacked slots.

title: Hungarian (Jonker-Volgenant) assignment — hungarian
code: hungarian(cost)

title: Hungarian (Jonker-Volgenant) assignment — hungarian
text: cost Square numeric cost matrix (cost[, j] = cost assigning row column j).

title: Hungarian (Jonker-Volgenant) assignment — hungarian
text: Integer vector res res[] 0-indexed column assigned row , minimising total cost.

what: Build the label + leader grobs for a mark — my_make_label
title: Build the label + leader grobs for a mark — my_make_label
text: Draw-time worker makeContent.shape_enc(): dilates cluster polygons buffer (box keep-), calls my_place_labels() placement, positions label box grobs builds leader polylines (anchor -> visible mask-boundary end, plus horizontal ledge con_type == "ledge").

title: Build the label + leader grobs for a mark — my_make_label
code: my_make_label(   labels,   dims,   polygons,   ghosts,   buffer,   con_type,   con_cap,   con_gp,   arrow,   simp_ratio = 0.001,   hardpad = unit(0, "pt"),   softpad = unit(0, "pt") )

title: Build the label + leader grobs for a mark — my_make_label
text: labels List label-box grobs (one per mark part). dims List measured label box sizes c(w, h) mm. polygons List cluster polygons (list(x, y)) mm. ghosts Points avoid (currently unused placer). buffer Grid unit: label.buffer polygon padding / box keep-. con_type Leader style: "ledge", "line", "box", "none". con_cap Numeric gap (mm) left leader end cluster. con_gp gpar connectors (per drawn label). arrow Optional grid::arrow connectors. simp_ratio Numeric polygon-simplification fraction (see simplify_outer()). hardpad Grid unit: label.hardpad hard box clearance. softpad Grid unit: label.softpad extra polish-target box spacing.

title: Build the label + leader grobs for a mark — my_make_label
text: gList-ready list: positioned label grobs followed connector grob.

what: Place labels at draw time (boundary-seed placer) — my_place_labels
title: Place labels at draw time (boundary-seed placer) — my_place_labels
text: Runs draw time panel's millimetre space: builds poles box-fit R-tree cluster polygons (cheap, ~20 ms ~40 clusters; expensive mask recomputed) calls placeLabels(). box-fit keep-uses dilated polygons poles, leader ends foreign-routing use true ones, leaders reach real outline.

title: Place labels at draw time (boundary-seed placer) — my_place_labels
code: my_place_labels(   rects,   polygons,   polygons_pad,   bounds,   simp_ratio = 0.001,   con_type = "ledge",   buffer = 0,   hardpad = 0,   softpad = 0 )

title: Place labels at draw time (boundary-seed placer) — my_place_labels
text: rects List measured label box sizes c(w, h) mm (zeroed entry = drawn). polygons List true cluster polygons (list(x, y)) mm. polygons_pad List polygons dilated label.buffer (box keep-). bounds Numeric c(width, height) panel mm. simp_ratio Numeric polygon-simplification fraction (see simplify_outer()). con_type Leader style: "ledge", "line", "box", "none". buffer Numeric label.buffer mm; overflow viewport inset . hardpad Numeric label.hardpad mm: hard box clearance folded every placement rectangle (seed slots, sweeps polish alike). softpad Numeric label.softpad mm: extra target box spacing polish aims , top hardpad.

title: Place labels at draw time (boundary-seed placer) — my_place_labels
text: list, one entry per input label: placed centre c(x, y) mm (NULL drawn), carrying attr(., "leaders") c(ex, ey, bx, , corner) per drawn label.

title: Place labels at draw time (boundary-seed placer) — my_place_labels
text: Note: cluster polygon single list(x, y) ring; mask holes resolved upstream generateMask(), layer treats every polygon one simple ring.

title: Single-move conflict sweep — oneMoveSweepKernel
text: Multi-pass Gauss-Seidel refinement. pass reorders labels current conflict vector (box-box, leader-leader, leader-box, leader length) descending, moves lexicographically best candidate versus current others (including staying put). Stops pass makes change.

title: Single-move conflict sweep — oneMoveSweepKernel
code: oneMoveSweepKernel(   cxmin,   cxmax,   cymin,   cymax,   ex,   ey,   tx,   ty,   len,   rows,   init,   maxpass )

title: Single-move conflict sweep — oneMoveSweepKernel
text: cxmin, cxmax, cymin, cymax Numeric padded box extents, one per candidate. ex, ey Numeric leader start (anchor) per candidate. tx, ty Numeric leader target (pole) per candidate. len Numeric ranking length per candidate. rows List integer vectors: 0-indexed candidate rows available label. init Integer vector: starting candidate index per label. maxpass Integer cap number sweeps.

title: Single-move conflict sweep — oneMoveSweepKernel
text: Integer vector: chosen candidate index per label.

title: Radial free-space label candidates — radialCandidates
text: cluster pole, marches ndir rays outward emits candidate box centre wherever box label's size (plus pad) cluster-free (BoxFit R-tree query): near edge every free interval every intfill along wide ones. Candidates may fall partly outside viewport – effective-length overflow term ranks , crowded label can take minimally-clipped edge slot instead far seed.

title: Radial free-space label candidates — radialCandidates
code: radialCandidates(   boxfit,   poi,   hw,   hh,   pad,   ndir,   step,   rstart,   rmax,   intfill,   dedup )

title: Radial free-space label candidates — radialCandidates
text: boxfit External pointer buildBoxFit(). poi K x 2 matrix cluster poles (ray origins). hw, hh Numeric per-label box half-sizes. pad Numeric hard box clearance added around box. ndir Integer number rays per pole. step Numeric radial step along ray. rstart, rmax Numeric first last radius searched. intfill Numeric spacing extra candidates along wide free interval. dedup Numeric grid size de-duplicating nearby candidates.

title: Radial free-space label candidates — radialCandidates
text: data.frame integer label (1-indexed) numeric cx, cy.

title: Enclosing polygon simplification — simplify_outer
text: Greedily removes small concave (inward) vertices polygon: vertex dropped triangle cuts area max_area. concave vertices removed simplified polygon ENCLOSES original, box-fit keep-built stays conservative. Used cut vertex counts placement.

title: Enclosing polygon simplification — simplify_outer
code: simplify_outer(poly, max_area, min_vertices = 4L)

title: Enclosing polygon simplification — simplify_outer
text: poly list numeric x, y (polygon vertices). max_area Numeric area threshold; vertices whose cut-triangle smaller removed. min_vertices Integer floor number vertices kept.

title: Enclosing polygon simplification — simplify_outer
text: list simplified numeric x, y.

title: Two-move conflict / length refinement — twoMoveSweepKernel
text: Per label (heaviest leader first), picks lexicographically best move (change box-box, leader-leader, leader-box conflicts, change length) applies improves. CONFLICT-FREE label uses length branch--bound: candidates length-ascending shorter c1 needs partner exactly one label currently sits slot (label partner – need conflict), length bound pruning length-increasing tail – exact per-step optimum. CONFLICTED label drops pruning searches pairs candidates (label's every label's), driving conflicts present input; conflicted labels pay cost.

title: Two-move conflict / length refinement — twoMoveSweepKernel
code: twoMoveSweepKernel(   cxmin,   cxmax,   cymin,   cymax,   ex,   ey,   tx,   ty,   len,   rows,   init,   maxpass,   sq )

title: Two-move conflict / length refinement — twoMoveSweepKernel
text: cxmin, cxmax, cymin, cymax Numeric padded box extents, one per candidate. ex, ey Numeric leader start (anchor) per candidate. tx, ty Numeric leader target (pole) per candidate. len Numeric ranking length per candidate. rows List integer vectors: 0-indexed candidate rows available label. init Integer vector: starting candidate index per label. maxpass Integer cap number passes. sq Logical; TRUE length objective uses squared length.

title: Two-move conflict / length refinement — twoMoveSweepKernel
text: Integer vector: chosen candidate index per label.
